teachingHint: This is Phase 5 review of the /ee/ sound your child learned in Phase 3. These are longer, more challenging words. Help your child spot 'ee' and blend the sounds: s-t-r-ee-t → street. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-t-r-ee-t → Spot the 'ee' digraph. Blend: str-ee-t → street
th-r-ee → Spot the 'ee' digraph and 'th' digraph. Blend: thr-ee → three
s-w-ee-t → Spot the 'ee' digraph. Blend: sw-ee-t → sweet
s-l-ee-p → Spot the 'ee' digraph. Blend: sl-ee-p → sleep

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Oh' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'h' is silent. We say 'oh'. Learn it by sight.

story: Oh! Look at the street! Three sweet cats sleep on the wall. Can you see all three?

teachingHint: More Phase 5 review of the /ee/ sound with even trickier words. Help your child spot 'ee' and blend carefully: s-p-ee-d → speed. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: t-ee-th → Spot the 'ee' digraph and 'th' digraph. Blend: t-ee-th → teeth
s-p-ee-d → Spot the 'ee' digraph. Blend: sp-ee-d → speed
ch-ee-s-e → Spot the 'ee' digraph and 'ch' digraph. Blend: ch-ee-se → cheese
s-qu-ee-z-e → Spot the 'ee' digraph and 'qu' digraph. Blend: squ-ee-ze → squeeze

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Their' doesn't sound like it looks. We say 'thair'. It means belonging to them. Learn it by sight.

story: Their teeth need a clean! No more cheese at speed. Squeeze the toothpaste and brush those teeth!

teachingHint: This is Phase 5 review of the /oa/ sound your child learned in Phase 3. These are longer words. Help your child spot 'oa' and blend: f-l-oa-t → float. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: f-l-oa-t → Spot the 'oa' digraph. Blend: fl-oa-t → float
t-oa-s-t → Spot the 'oa' digraph. Blend: t-oa-st → toast
c-oa-ch → Spot the 'oa' digraph and 'ch' digraph. Blend: c-oa-ch → coach
r-oa-s-t → Spot the 'oa' digraph. Blend: r-oa-st → roast

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'People' doesn't sound like it looks. We say 'pee-pul'. Learn it by sight.

story: People love toast! The coach said to roast it, then let it float in the air to cool down.

teachingHint: More Phase 5 review of the /oa/ sound with trickier words. Help your child spot 'oa' and blend: th-r-oa-t → throat. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: th-r-oa-t → Spot the 'oa' digraph and 'th' digraph. Blend: thr-oa-t → throat
g-r-oa-n → Spot the 'oa' digraph. Blend: gr-oa-n → groan
f-oa-m → Spot the 'oa' digraph. Blend: f-oa-m → foam
s-oa-k → Spot the 'oa' digraph. Blend: s-oa-k → soak

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Mr' is short for 'Mister'. We say 'mis-ter'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mr Toad had a sore throat. He let out a groan! He had a soak in the foam bath to feel better.

teachingHint: This is Phase 5 review of the /ai/ sound your child learned in Phase 3. These are longer, more advanced words. Help your child spot 'ai' and blend: s-n-ai-l → snail. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-n-ai-l → Spot the 'ai' digraph. Blend: sn-ai-l → snail
ch-ai-n → Spot the 'ai' digraph and 'ch' digraph. Blend: ch-ai-n → chain
b-r-ai-n → Spot the 'ai' digraph. Blend: br-ai-n → brain
p-l-ai-n → Spot the 'ai' digraph. Blend: pl-ai-n → plain

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Mrs' is short for 'Missus'. We say 'mis-iz'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mrs Snail had a big brain! She used a chain to pull a plain box. What could be inside?

teachingHint: More Phase 5 review of the /ai/ sound with multi-syllable words. Help your child spot 'ai' in longer words: a-f-r-ai-d → afraid. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: c-l-ai-m → Spot the 'ai' digraph. Blend: cl-ai-m → claim
a-f-r-ai-d → Spot the 'ai' digraph. Blend: a-fr-ai-d → afraid
c-o-m-p-l-ai-n → Spot the 'ai' digraph in this long word. Blend: com-pl-ai-n → complain
e-x-p-l-ai-n → Spot the 'ai' digraph in this long word. Blend: ex-pl-ai-n → explain

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'ed' ending sounds like /t/ here. We say 'lookt'. Learn it by sight.

story: She looked afraid! "I claim this is not fair!" she said. "Let me explain why I complain."

teachingHint: This is Phase 5 review of the /igh/ sound your child learned in Phase 3. These words use consonant clusters with the 'igh' trigraph. Help your child blend: f-l-igh-t → flight. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: f-l-igh-t → Spot the 'igh' trigraph. Blend: fl-igh-t → flight
b-r-igh-t → Spot the 'igh' trigraph. Blend: br-igh-t → bright
f-r-igh-t → Spot the 'igh' trigraph. Blend: fr-igh-t → fright
k-n-igh-t → The 'k' is silent! Spot the 'igh' trigraph. Blend: n-igh-t → knight

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'ed' ending sounds like /d/ here. We say 'calld'. Learn it by sight.

story: The knight called out from the bright flight deck. "What a fright!" he said as the flight took off.

teachingHint: More Phase 5 review of the /igh/ trigraph with longer words. Help your child spot 'igh' and blend: m-i-d-n-igh-t → midnight. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: m-i-d-n-igh-t → Spot the 'igh' trigraph in this long word. Blend: mid-n-igh-t → midnight
h-igh-l-igh-t → Two 'igh' trigraphs! Blend: h-igh-l-igh-t → highlight
m-igh-t → Spot the 'igh' trigraph. Blend: m-igh-t → might
s-igh-t → Spot the 'igh' trigraph. Blend: s-igh-t → sight

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'ed' ending sounds like /t/ here. We say 'askt'. Learn it by sight.

story: She asked if they might see a sight at midnight. "I will highlight it for you!" she said with delight.

teachingHint: This is Phase 5 review of the /oi/ sound your child learned in Phase 3. These are more advanced words. Help your child spot 'oi' and blend: ch-oi-c-e → choice. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: ch-oi-c-e → Spot the 'oi' digraph and 'ch' digraph. Blend: ch-oi-ce → choice
v-oi-c-e → Spot the 'oi' digraph. Blend: v-oi-ce → voice
m-oi-s-t → Spot the 'oi' digraph. Blend: m-oi-st → moist
n-oi-s-e → Spot the 'oi' digraph. Blend: n-oi-se → noise

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'oul' sounds like /oo/. We say 'cud'. The 'l' is silent. Learn it by sight.

story: "Could you keep the noise down?" said a moist voice. "It is my choice to be quiet!"

teachingHint: More Phase 5 review of the /oi/ sound with multi-syllable words. Help your child spot 'oi' in longer words: p-oi-s-o-n → poison. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: p-oi-s-o-n → Spot the 'oi' digraph in this two-syllable word. Blend: p-oi-son → poison
b-oi-l-i-ng → Spot the 'oi' digraph and 'ng' digraph. Blend: b-oi-l-i-ng → boiling
ch-oi-c-e → Spot the 'oi' digraph and 'ch' digraph. Blend: ch-oi-ce → choice
n-oi-s-e → Spot the 'oi' digraph. Blend: n-oi-se → noise

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'People' doesn't sound like it looks. We say 'pee-pul'. Learn it by sight.

story: People made a big noise! The water was boiling and the poison had to go. "Good choice!" they said.
